findings.claim: CeCILL FREE SOFTWARE LICENSE AGREEMENT
findings.evidence: FluidSim is distributed under the CeCILL License, a GPL compatible French license, confirmed via PyPI and GitHub.

findings.claim: Documentation at https://fluidsim.readthedocs.io/
findings.evidence: Fluidsim documentation confirmed at fluidsim.readthedocs.io, currently version 0.9.0.

findings.claim: Over 800,000 economic time series from over 100 sources
findings.evidence: FRED provides access to 840,000+ economic time series from 118 sources per official documentation. The 800,000+ claim is slightly conservative but accurate.

findings.claim: API base URL: https://api.stlouisfed.org/fred
findings.evidence: Confirmed via official FRED API documentation at fred.stlouisfed.org/docs/api/fred/

findings.claim: E-utilities rate limits: 3 requests/second without API key, 10 requests/second with API key
findings.evidence: NCBI official documentation confirms 3 req/s without key and 10 req/s with API key for E-utilities.

findings.claim: BRCA1 gene ID is 672
findings.evidence: NCBI Gene confirms BRCA1 BRCA1 DNA repair associated [Homo sapiens] has Gene ID 672.

findings.claim: Datasets API rate limit: 5 requests/second without API key
findings.evidence: Could not find specific rate limit documentation for the NCBI Datasets API without a key. E-utilities rate limits are well-documented (3/s without, 10/s with) but the Datasets API may have different limits.

findings.claim: OpenRouter model ID: black-forest-labs/flux.2-pro for FLUX.2 Pro image generation
findings.evidence: Confirmed via OpenRouter's model page at openrouter.ai/black-forest-labs/flux.2-pro.

findings.claim: OpenRouter model ID: google/gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview (Nano Banana 2)
findings.evidence: Confirmed via OpenRouter's model page. The model is officially named 'Nano Banana 2 (Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview)' at openrouter.ai/google/gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview.

findings.claim: GitHub repository at https://github.com/databio/geniml
findings.evidence: Repository confirmed at github.com/databio/geniml, described as 'Genomic interval machine learning'.

findings.claim: StarSpace from Facebook Research used for BEDspace at https://github.com/facebookresearch/StarSpace
findings.evidence: StarSpace repository confirmed at github.com/facebookresearch/StarSpace, described as 'Learning embeddings for classification, retrieval and ranking'.

findings.claim: BSD-2-Clause license
findings.evidence: Could not confirm specific license from search results. The GitHub repo exists but license details were not returned in search results.

findings.claim: GEO contains over 264,000 studies with more than 8 million samples (264,928+ series, 8,068,632+ samples)
findings.evidence: Official NCBI GEO documentation states 'over 200,000 studies and 6.5 million samples'. The SKILL.md claims 264,928+ series and 8,068,632+ samples are significantly higher than documented counts. These may represent more recent numbers but could not be verified as of the search date.
findings.correct_info: NCBI GEO documentation references 'over 200,000 studies and 6.5 million samples'. The exact current numbers may differ but the SKILL.md figures could not be independently confirmed.

findings.claim: GPL570 is the Affymetrix Human Genome U133 Plus 2.0 Array
findings.evidence: Confirmed via NCBI GEO Accession viewer at ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/geo/query/acc.cgi?acc=GPL570. Platform contains 54,675 probes.

findings.claim: Barrett et al. (2013) Nucleic Acids Research as the GEO citation
findings.evidence: Barrett et al. 2013 is a commonly cited GEO reference paper in Nucleic Acids Research. PMID: 23193258.

findings.claim: Sentinel-2 band assignments: B04=Red, B08=NIR
findings.evidence: Confirmed via ESA/Sentinel Hub documentation. B04 is Red (0.64-0.67 μm), B08 is NIR (842 nm, 10m resolution).

findings.claim: NDVI formula: (NIR - Red) / (NIR + Red)
findings.evidence: Standard NDVI formula confirmed. Sentinel Hub documentation: NDVI = (B8-B4)/(B8+B4).

findings.claim: BSD-3-Clause license, extends pandas with shapely
findings.evidence: GeoPandas is a well-known Python library under BSD-3-Clause license that extends pandas DataFrames with spatial operations via Shapely geometries.

findings.claim: Citation: Luebbert, L. & Pachter, L. (2023). Efficient querying of genomic reference databases with gget. Bioinformatics. https://doi.org/10.1093/bioinformatics/btac836
findings.evidence: Confirmed via Oxford Academic. Published in Bioinformatics, Volume 39, Issue 1, January 2023, btac836. PMID: 36610989.

findings.claim: GitHub repository at https://github.com/pachterlab/gget
findings.evidence: Confirmed. Repository exists at github.com/pachterlab/gget with documentation at pachterlab.github.io/gget/.

findings.claim: GitHub repository at https://github.com/databio/gtars, Rust toolkit for genomic intervals
findings.evidence: Confirmed at github.com/databio/gtars. Described as 'Performance-critical tools to manipulate, analyze, and process genomic interval data'. Also available on crates.io.

findings.claim: EFO_0001360 corresponds to type 2 diabetes
findings.evidence: Confirmed via EBI Ontology Lookup Service. EFO_0001360 is type 2 diabetes mellitus (now obsolete, replaced by MONDO_0005148, but the mapping is correct).

findings.claim: Citation: Sollis E, et al. (2023) PMID: 37953337
findings.evidence: PMID 37953337 is actually the 'Ensembl 2024' paper (Nucleic Acids Res, DOI: 10.1093/nar/gkad1049), NOT the Sollis GWAS Catalog paper. The correct PMID for Sollis et al. (2023) 'The NHGRI-EBI GWAS Catalog: knowledgebase and deposition resource' is PMID 36350656.
findings.correct_info: The correct citation is: Sollis E, et al. (2023) The NHGRI-EBI GWAS Catalog: knowledgebase and deposition resource. Nucleic Acids Research. PMID: 36350656. DOI: 10.1093/nar/gkac1010.

findings.claim: GWAS Catalog API base URL: https://www.ebi.ac.uk/gwas/rest/api
findings.evidence: Confirmed via official EBI GWAS Catalog API documentation at ebi.ac.uk/gwas/rest/docs/api.

findings.claim: OFR Hedge Fund Monitor API base URL: https://data.financialresearch.gov/hf/v1
findings.evidence: Confirmed via official OFR documentation at financialresearch.gov/hedge-fund-monitor/api/. The API is free and requires no registration.

findings.claim: Four datasets: fpf (SEC Form PF), tff (CFTC), scoos (FRB), ficc (FICC Sponsored Repo)
findings.evidence: Confirmed. OFR documentation lists four data sources: SEC Form PF, CFTC Commitments of Traders, FRB Senior Credit Officer Opinion Survey, and FICC Sponsored Repo data.

findings.claim: Apache-2.0 license
findings.evidence: Confirmed via GitHub repository at github.com/histolab/histolab, licensed under Apache-2.0.

findings.claim: Python library for processing whole slide images in digital pathology
findings.evidence: Confirmed. Published paper: 'histolab: A Python library for reproducible Digital Pathology preprocessing with automated testing' (SoftwareX, 2022).

findings.claim: 220,945 metabolite entries in HMDB version 5.0
findings.evidence: Confirmed via HMDB website and publication (Wishart et al., 2022, Nucleic Acids Research). HMDB 5.0 contains 220,945 metabolite entries.

findings.claim: HMDB v5.0 current version date 2023-07-01
findings.evidence: HMDB 5.0 was published as 'HMDB 5.0: the Human Metabolome Database for 2022' in Nucleic Acids Research (published online January 4, 2022, PMID: 34986597). The version date '2023-07-01' in the SKILL.md does not match the actual publication year of 2022.
findings.correct_info: HMDB 5.0 was published in January 2022 (Nucleic Acids Res, 2022, 50(D1), D622-D631). The specific version date may refer to a data update, but the paper was published in 2022, not 2023.

findings.claim: HMDB website at https://www.hmdb.ca/
findings.evidence: Confirmed. The Human Metabolome Database is hosted at hmdb.ca.

findings.claim: GitHub repository at https://github.com/ChicagoHAI/hypothesis-generation
findings.evidence: Confirmed at github.com/ChicagoHAI/hypothesis-generation. Described as the official repository for HypoGeniC and HypoRefine.

findings.claim: 8.97% improvement over few-shot baselines and 15.75% improvement over literature-only approaches
findings.evidence: Confirmed via arxiv paper 2410.17309 (Literature Meets Data). The paper reports '8.97% over few-shot, 15.75% over literature-based alone, and 3.37% over data-driven alone'.

findings.claim: arxiv paper 2410.17309: Liu et al. (2024) 'Literature Meets Data: A Synergistic Approach to Hypothesis Generation'
findings.evidence: Confirmed at arxiv.org/abs/2410.17309. Authors: Haokun Liu, Yangqiaoyu Zhou, Mingxuan Li, Chenfei Yuan, and Chenhao Tan.

notes: fluidsim: Mostly procedural with verifiable library info. All claims supported.
fred-economic-data: Well-documented public API. All claims supported.
gene-database: NCBI E-utilities claims verified. Datasets API rate limit unverifiable.
geniml: Repository and dependencies verified. License unverifiable from search alone.
geo-database: GEO statistics (264K+ series, 8M+ samples) could not be confirmed — official docs cite 200K+ studies and 6.5M samples. Numbers may be more recent but are flagged.
geomaster: Procedural geospatial guide. Spectral index formulas and band assignments verified.
geopandas: Procedural. Well-known Python library, basic claims trivially true.
get-available-resources: Procedural utility skill. No factual claims to verify.
gget: Citation and repository fully verified.
gtars: Repository verified at databio/gtars on both GitHub and crates.io.
gwas-database: CRITICAL — PMID 37953337 is wrong (it's the Ensembl 2024 paper, not the Sollis GWAS Catalog paper). Correct PMID is 36350656.
hmdb-database: Metabolite count verified. Version date '2023-07-01' is questionable — HMDB 5.0 was published in 2022, though data updates may have occurred in 2023.
hypogenic: All claims including performance metrics verified against the arxiv paper.
hypothesis-generation: Procedural methodology guide. No factual claims to verify.
